chapter_context.summary: Presents a radiant vision of the desert blossoming, the weak strengthened, the blind and deaf healed, and the redeemed returning to Zion along the Highway of Holiness.

outline.text: Glory of Lebanon Given to Desert; Strengthening the Weak
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Glory of Lebanon Given to Desert; Strengthening the Weak (35:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Healing of Blind, Deaf, Lame, and Dumb in Joyful Springs
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Healing of Blind, Deaf, Lame, and Dumb in Joyful Springs (35:5-7), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Way of Holiness and Return of Redeemed to Zion
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Way of Holiness and Return of Redeemed to Zion (35:8-10), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 35, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
